frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: noisy evidence accumulates over time to a bound terminating the decision

frameworks.name: bounded accumulation / threshold-crossing
frameworks.one_line: extends accumulator account by reading drift-diffusion on single trials

frameworks.name: population coding in LIP
frameworks.one_line: weighted sum of LIP neurons indexes the decision variable

core_question: Can the stochastic drift-diffusion signal underlying a single perceptual decision be observed directly in cortical activity?
key_assumptions: noisy evidence is integrated over time toward a termination bound
trial-to-trial choice and RT variability arise from a stochastic diffusion component
a scalar decision variable can be read from a weighted sum of LIP population activity
manipulations: simultaneous large-scale recording of hundreds of LIP neurons within single trials
key_findings: a scalar derived from weighted LIP population activity exhibits both deterministic drift and stochastic diffusion within single trials
this single-trial signal carries the unobserved stochastic component thought to drive choice and RT variability
the signal approximates the quantity at which decisions are terminated
interpretations: LIP population activity directly instantiates the drift-diffusion decision variable on individual decisions, not just in trial averages
supports drift-diffusion as the mechanistic, not merely descriptive, account of perceptual choice termination
